L1.area: Network signaling and radio access management message handling

L2.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the processing of wireless network handover signaling messages. When certain message fields reference UE context associations or radio connection references that are not initialized or have been released, the process dereferences nil pointers without validation, causing a panic that crashes the service and disrupts all connected subscribers.

L3.area: NGAP handover signaling handlers in the access and mobility management function
L3.description: A null pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the NGAP handover signaling message handlers. When processing certain handover-related messages, the code accesses UE context associations and radio connection references without checking for nil, causing a panic when these references are unset during abnormal handover scenarios. An attacker who can force a gNodeB to send specific handover messages can crash the core network process, disrupting service for all connected subscribers.

ground_truth.reason: Multiple NGAP handover handler functions dereference nil pointers when accessing UE context associations (AmfUe()) and radio connection references (Radio) without null checks, causing a process panic when these are unset.
